{"query":"Android 17 Compose-first UI release details","model":"mako","response_time_ms":2329,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://developer.android.com/blog/posts/android-17-is-here","title":"Android 17 is Here  |  Android Developers' Blog","content":"To that end, Android development is now Compose-first.
All new Android APIs, libraries, tools, and developer guidance will be built exclusively for Jetpack Compose.
With Android 17, we're making it safer to enter passwords, PINs, and other secrets when using a physical keyboard by no longer showing the last typed character by default.
Note: You'll need to update your CameraX version to either 1.5.2 or 1.6.0+ to avoid a crash related to an added dynamic range mode on Android 17 devices.
Android 17 is available in beta on handset, tablet, and foldable form factors from partners including Honor, iQOO, Lenovo, OnePlus, OPPO, Realme, Sharp, vivo, and Xiaomi.
For the best development experience with Android 17, we recommend that you use the latest Canary build of Android Studio Quail."},{"url":"https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/platforms/android/android-17-features/","title":"Android 17 has new features for productivity, gaming and security","content":"You can now record your reactions over videos and keep your data safer with smarter security tools.
It’s rolling out first to Pixel today, followed by other eligible Android devices throughout 2026.
Under the hood, Android 17 introduces app memory limits to ensure apps never use too much RAM, helping improve your device’s overall performance and battery life."},{"url":"https://www.phonearena.com/android-17-release-date-features-news-compatible-phones","title":"Android 17 expected release date, supported devices and must-know features - PhoneArena","content":"Android 17 expected release date, supported devices and must-know features - PhoneArena
- Android 17 was officially announced on May 12, 2026, during Google's The Android Show.
Currently, Android 17's latest release is QPR4 Beta 1.
Google showed many of the new Android 17 features during The Android Show, including Gemini Intelligence, custom widgets, Rambler, Pause Point, and creative tools.
These personalized widgets will still adhere to Google's standard Material Expressive design language, ensuring they look native to the ecosystem.
Android 17 may have been announced, but it's still in beta, and not all features were disclosed by Google during The Android Show.
Features that will carry over from Android 16 QPR1:"},{"url":"https://www.androidcentral.com/apps-software/android-os/android-17","title":"Android 17: Everything you need to know | Android Central","content":"Google rolled out the initial Android 17 beta in February, and we got the Beta 3 build at the end of March, followed by Beta 4 in April, and the QPR1 Beta 2 build in May.
Google showed off plenty of new features coming to Android at The Android Show ahead of I/O, and on June 16, it rolled out the stable version of Android 17.
What's notable is that non-Google devices are now eligible to install the Android 17 beta, with Samsung, Honor, OPPO, Xiaomi and other manufacturers included in the Android beta program.
Android 14 was Upside Down Cake, Android 15 was Vanilla Ice Cream, and with Android 16, Google switched over to Baklava, and Android 17 gets Cinnamon Bun as the codename.
Android 17 will see a tweaked design of its UI that's meant to mimic a frosted glass look, similar to what iOS has with Liquid Glass."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Android_17","title":"Android 17 - Wikipedia","content":"The system will ignore screen orientation and resizability restrictions, completely removing the option to opt-out of big-screen orientation and resizing limitations that was previously introduced in Android 16.
"Google is now rolling out Android 17 Beta 2 for Pixel"."}]}

Google just gave Android developers one of the most powerful UI paradigms the platform has ever seen with Remote Compose and Android 17. Remote Compose is the official server-driven UI framework in Jetpack Compose by Google.
